_comment: Manually-maintained inputs for the MB Economic Update tab. Edit this file when these values change, then commit. Numbers that auto-refresh from StatsCan/CMHC/CREA are NOT here — only the values with no clean public feed. Build step: r/15_build_economic_update.R folds this into web/public/data/economy/economic-update.json.

paragraphs: The most recent TD Economic Forecast highlights a steep downgrade to their 2026 average Canadian home resale and price-growth forecast due to weak performance in Q4 2025 and Q1 2026. They predict sales will likely take most of the year to recover first-quarter losses, citing a subdued economy, heightened uncertainty, and cost-of-living pressures.

TD predicts a lower growth rate in Manitoba's real GDP in 2025 on downgraded expectations for utilities and trade-exposed industries like manufacturing. Manitoba is noted as one of the worst-hit provinces from the U.S. trade war, with falling exports of energy, agricultural machinery, and pharmaceutical products.

TD expects the construction sector to slow its growth this year after gains in 2025, with weakening non-residential investment projected in 2026. Positives noted include increased canola exports as Chinese tariffs ease, potential gains in the goods and utility sector, and new international markets that saw an 8% increase in shipments last year.

The forecast notes a significant slowdown in public spending for 2026 that will limit growth, with public-debt levels expected to constrain Manitoba government spending. Employment was up 1% year-over-year in February — higher than the Canadian average — though this too is anticipated to slow in 2026.
